response.justification: Bogdan brings strong staff-level seniority, proven TypeScript expertise, and is currently working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at Google—directly relevant to the role's technical scope. However, the resume lacks demonstrated experience building AI-driven solutions using LLMs, vector databases, or RAG systems, which are core requirements. His transition into GenAI work appears recent (April 2025), making it unclear whether his current role involves hands-on AI system development or primarily infrastructure tooling. An interview should focus on the depth and scope of his AI-agent work at Google and prior involvement with large language models.
strengths: Current Staff Engineer at Google working on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks—directly aligned with key technologies
18+ years of full-stack software development with proven ability to ship production systems, mentor teams, and lead technical initiatives across backend, DevOps, and infrastructure
Strong TypeScript/Node.js expertise and AWS cloud platform experience with demonstrated mastery of low-latency systems, microservices, and high-performance infrastructure
concerns: No documented experience building AI-driven solutions, working with vector databases, or implementing RAG architectures—core job requirements unfulfilled in resume
Python not listed among technical skills despite being a primary language for AI/ML work; polyglot background raises questions about depth in AI/ML stacks versus breadth across languages
AI-agent infrastructure work at Google is very recent (started April 2025) and insufficiently detailed to assess hands-on involvement with LLMs, prompt optimization, or multi-agent system frameworks

key_factors.factor: Recent AI-agent infrastructure role at Google
Lack of documented AI/ML and vector-database experience
